title: Smart collection schema generation with item schema inference
description:
When generating a schema from a collection entry with `pudl schema new --from <collection> --collection`, the app should:

1. For each item in the collection, run schema inference to find matching schemas
2. Group items by their inferred schema:
   - Items matching existing schemas (e.g., #Ec2Instance) → use those
   - Items with no match → generate new item schema(s)
3. Generate the collection schema as a union of all matched/generated item schemas:
   ```cue
   #Ec2InstanceCollection: [...(#Ec2Instance | #SomeOtherInstance)]
   ```

## Current Behavior
The --collection flag generates an object schema with schema_type: "collection" in metadata, but the schema is structurally an object, not an array. This is incorrect - collection schemas should be structurally arrays.

## Expected Behavior
- If all items match existing schemas → generate only the collection schema referencing them
- If some items don't match → generate new item schemas AND the collection schema
- Heterogeneous collections should produce union types: `[...(#TypeA | #TypeB)]`

## Example
```
pudl schema new --from govim-nupab --path aws/ec2:#Ec2InstanceCollection --collection
```

If govim-nupab contains 5 EC2 instances and #Ec2Instance already exists and matches all items:
```cue
#Ec2InstanceCollection: [...#Ec2Instance]
```

If 3 items match #Ec2Instance and 2 don't match anything:
```cue
#Ec2InstanceCollection: [...(#Ec2Instance | #Ec2InstanceVariant)]
```
(where #Ec2InstanceVariant is auto-generated for the unmatched items)

## Implementation Notes
- Use existing schema inference from internal/inference package
- Include all candidate schemas regardless of confidence level (revisit if needed)
- May need to generate multiple files if new item schemas are created
- Consider how to name auto-generated item schemas

title: Define TypePattern struct and pattern registry
description:
Create the core type pattern infrastructure in a new package internal/typepattern/.

FILES TO CREATE:
- internal/typepattern/pattern.go - Core types and registry
- internal/typepattern/registry.go - Pattern registration and lookup

TYPEPATTERN STRUCT:
Define TypePattern with these fields:
- Name (string): Identifies pattern like kubernetes, aws-ec2
- Ecosystem (string): Groups related patterns like kubernetes, aws, gitlab
- RequiredFields ([]string): Fields that must be present
- OptionalFields ([]string): Fields that boost confidence
- FieldValues (map[string][]string): Field name to expected values
- TypeExtractor (func): Extracts specific type from matched data
- ImportMapper (func): Maps type to CUE import path
- MetadataDefaults (func): Returns default PUDL metadata
- Priority (int): Higher = checked first

DETECTEDTYPE STRUCT:
- Pattern (*TypePattern)
- TypeID (string): e.g. batch/v1:Job
- ImportPath (string): e.g. cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/batch/v1
- Definition (string): e.g. Job
- Confidence (float64)

PUDLMETADATA STRUCT:
- SchemaType, ResourceType, CascadePriority, IdentityFields, TrackedFields

REGISTRY INTERFACE:
- NewRegistry() creates registry with default patterns
- Register(p *TypePattern) adds pattern
- Detect(data map[string]interface{}) tries all patterns, returns best match
- GetPatternsByEcosystem(ecosystem string) returns filtered patterns

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- TypePattern struct defined with all fields
- DetectedType result struct defined
- PudlMetadata struct defined
- Registry with Register, Detect, GetPatternsByEcosystem methods
- Patterns sorted by Priority when detecting
- Detection returns highest confidence match
- Unit tests for registry operations

NOTES:
- This is foundational - other tasks depend on this
- Keep it simple, no external dependencies
- Patterns will be registered by ecosystem-specific tasks

Natural complement to import functionality - enables data workflows.

title: Add CLI command: pudl schema generate-type
description:
Add CLI command for manually generating schemas from detected types.

NEW COMMAND:
pudl schema generate-type --kind <kind> --api-version <version> [--ecosystem <name>]

EXAMPLES:
pudl schema generate-type --kind Job --api-version batch/v1
pudl schema generate-type --kind Deployment --api-version apps/v1
pudl schema generate-type --ecosystem aws --type ec2:Instance

FILES TO MODIFY:
- cmd/schema.go - Add generate-type subcommand

COMMAND BEHAVIOR:
1. Parse ecosystem/type information from flags
2. Look up pattern in registry
3. Generate schema using schemagen.GenerateFromDetectedType
4. Save to appropriate location
5. Run cue mod tidy if needed (to fetch dependencies)

FLAGS:
--kind        K8s resource kind (e.g., Job, Deployment)
--api-version K8s API version (e.g., batch/v1, apps/v1)
--ecosystem   Ecosystem name (kubernetes, aws, gitlab) - defaults to kubernetes
--type        Generic type identifier for non-K8s (e.g., ec2:Instance)
--force       Overwrite existing schema
--dry-run     Print generated schema without saving

OUTPUT:
Generated schema: ~/.pudl/schema/pudl/k8s/job.cue
  Package: k8s
  Definition: #Job
  Extends: cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/batch/v1.#Job

DRY-RUN OUTPUT:
Print the full CUE schema content to stdout

ERROR HANDLING:
- Unknown kind/api-version: suggest similar known types
- Schema already exists: require --force flag
- Missing dependencies: suggest running pudl module tidy

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Command parses K8s-style --kind/--api-version flags
- Command supports generic --ecosystem/--type flags
- Schema generated and saved correctly
- --dry-run prints without saving
- --force overwrites existing
- Helpful error messages for unknown types
- Integration with cue mod tidy for dependencies

DEPENDS ON:
- pudl-49i (TypePattern struct and registry)
- Schema generation task
- At least one pattern implementation

title: Add pudl schema reinfer command to re-run schema inference on existing data
description:
Currently there is no way to re-run schema inference on data that has already been imported. This is needed when:

- New schemas have been added and you want existing data to match against them
- Schema cascade priorities have been modified
- You want to batch-update schema assignments without going through interactive review

**Proposed command:** \Manage CUE schemas used for data validation and organization in PUDL.

Schemas are organized by packages (aws, k8s, unknown, etc.) and stored in the
schema repository at ~/.pudl/schema/. Each schema is a CUE file that defines
the structure and validation rules for imported data.

Available subcommands:
- list:   Show available schemas organized by package
- add:    Add a new schema file to the repository
- status: Show uncommitted changes in the schema repository
- commit: Commit schema changes to version control
- log:    Show commit history of schema changes

Examples:
    pudl schema list                           # List all schemas
    pudl schema list --package aws             # List schemas in aws package
    pudl schema add aws.rds-instance my.cue    # Add new schema to aws package
    pudl schema status                         # Show uncommitted changes
    pudl schema commit -m "Add RDS schema"     # Commit changes
    pudl schema log                            # Show recent commits

Usage:
  pudl schema [flags]
  pudl schema [command]

Available Commands:
  add         Add a new schema to the repository
  commit      Commit schema changes to version control
  edit        Open a schema file in your editor
  list        List available schemas
  log         Show commit history of schema changes
  new         Generate a new schema from imported data
  review      Interactively review and improve schema assignments
  status      Show uncommitted changes in the schema repository

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for schema

Use "pudl schema [command] --help" for more information about a command.

**Flags:**
- \ - Re-infer schemas for all catalog entries
- \ - Re-infer schema for a specific entry
- \ - Re-infer only entries currently assigned to this schema (e.g., \)
- \ - Re-infer only entries from a specific origin
- \ - Show what would change without applying updates
- \ - Apply changes without confirmation prompt

**Example usage:**
\\\

**Implementation notes:**
- Use the existing \ from \
- Use \ from \ to update entries
- Filter logic can be borrowed from \
- Should show a summary of changes (X entries updated, Y unchanged, Z failed)

**Related commands:**
- \🔍 Schema Review Workflow
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
🚀 Starting new schema review session...

📊 Review Preview:
  Total items matching criteria: 6
  Items to review (max): 6

📋 Schema Breakdown:
  pudl.schemas/pudl/unknown@v0:#CatchAll: 5 items
  pudl.schemas/collections/collections:#Collection: 1 items

Proceed with review? [y/N]: Review cancelled. - Interactive version (already exists)
- \ - Validates against current schema (already exists)

title: Add schema generation for detected types
description:
Extend internal/schemagen/generator.go to generate schemas for detected types that extend canonical CUE schemas.

NEW FUNCTION:
Add GenerateFromDetectedType to Generator:

func (g *Generator) GenerateFromDetectedType(
    detected *typepattern.DetectedType,
    sampleData interface{},
) (*GenerateResult, error)

BEHAVIOR:
1. If detected.ImportPath is set (canonical schema available):
   - Generate schema that imports and extends the canonical type
   - Add PUDL metadata from detected.Pattern.MetadataDefaults
   
2. If detected.ImportPath is empty (no canonical schema):
   - Fall back to existing Generate() behavior
   - Still use MetadataDefaults for better defaults

GENERATED SCHEMA FORMAT (with canonical import):
package pudl

import batch \cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/batch/v1\

#Job: batch.#Job & {
    _pudl: {
        schema_type:      \kubernetes\
        resource_type:    \k8s.batch.job\
        cascade_priority: 90
        identity_fields: [\metadata.name\, \metadata.namespace\]
        tracked_fields: [\status.succeeded\, \status.failed\]
    }
}

SCHEMA FILE LOCATION:
Save to ~/.pudl/schema/pudl/<ecosystem>/<definition>.cue
e.g., ~/.pudl/schema/pudl/k8s/job.cue

HELPER FUNCTION:
Add generateCUEContentWithImport for import-based schemas:
- Takes import path, definition name, and PUDL metadata
- Generates proper CUE syntax with import statement
- Handles import aliasing (batch for batch/v1, apps for apps/v1, etc.)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- GenerateFromDetectedType creates schema extending canonical type
- Proper CUE import syntax generated
- PUDL metadata block included with defaults from pattern
- Schema file saved to correct location
- Falls back to standard generation when no import path
- Generated schema validates with cue vet
- Unit tests for generation with and without imports

DEPENDS ON:
- pudl-49i (TypePattern struct and registry)

INTEGRATION NOTE:
This task creates the generation capability. Integration with import flow is separate task.

title: Add integration tests for type detection and auto-generation
description:
Create comprehensive integration tests for the type detection and auto-schema generation feature.

TEST FILE:
test/integration/type_detection_test.go

TEST SCENARIOS:

1. K8S RESOURCE DETECTION:
- Import K8s Job JSON without existing schema
- Verify type detected as kubernetes/batch/v1:Job
- Verify schema generated at pudl/k8s/job.cue
- Verify schema extends cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/batch/v1.#Job
- Verify data assigned to new schema on re-inference

2. K8S COLLECTION DETECTION:
- Import array of K8s Pods
- Verify each item detected and schema generated
- Verify collection assigned correctly

3. AWS EC2 DETECTION:
- Import AWS describe-instances response
- Verify detected as aws/ec2:Instance
- Verify schema generated with InstanceId identity field

4. GITLAB CI DETECTION:
- Import GitLab CI YAML (as parsed JSON/map)
- Verify detected as gitlab-ci:Pipeline
- Verify schema generated extending gitlabci.#Pipeline

5. MIXED COLLECTION:
- Import collection with K8s and AWS resources
- Verify each item gets appropriate schema

6. NO MATCH FALLBACK:
- Import unknown data structure
- Verify falls back to catchall schema
- No erroneous schema generation

7. EXISTING SCHEMA PRIORITY:
- Create custom K8s Job schema first
- Import K8s Job
- Verify uses existing schema, not auto-generated

8. CLI GENERATE-TYPE:
- Run pudl schema generate-type --kind Deployment --api-version apps/v1
- Verify schema file created
- Verify validates with cue vet

TEST DATA:
Create test fixtures in test/integration/testdata/type_detection/:
- k8s_job.json
- k8s_pods.json (array)
- aws_ec2_instances.json
- gitlab_ci.yaml (or .json)
- unknown_data.json

HELPER FUNCTIONS:
- setupCleanPudlEnv() - Fresh ~/.pudl for each test
- assertSchemaGenerated(path, extends) - Check schema file
- assertSchemaValidates(path) - Run cue vet

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All 8 test scenarios pass
- Tests are isolated (clean PUDL env each time)
- Test fixtures are realistic
- Tests run in reasonable time (< 30s total)
- Tests documented with clear descriptions

DEPENDS ON:

title: Implement Kubernetes type pattern
description:
Implement the Kubernetes type detection pattern in internal/typepattern/kubernetes.go.

DETECTION LOGIC:
Match when data has: apiVersion, kind, metadata fields

TYPE EXTRACTION:
Combine apiVersion and kind into type ID, e.g.:
- apiVersion: batch/v1, kind: Job -> batch/v1:Job
- apiVersion: apps/v1, kind: Deployment -> apps/v1:Deployment
- apiVersion: v1, kind: Pod -> v1:Pod

IMPORT MAPPING:
Map type ID to CUE import path. Build mapping for common K8s resources:
- batch/v1:Job -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/batch/v1, Job
- batch/v1:CronJob -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/batch/v1, CronJob
- apps/v1:Deployment -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/apps/v1, Deployment
- apps/v1:StatefulSet -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/apps/v1, StatefulSet
- apps/v1:DaemonSet -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/apps/v1, DaemonSet
- apps/v1:ReplicaSet -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/apps/v1, ReplicaSet
- v1:Pod -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/core/v1, Pod
- v1:Service -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/core/v1, Service
- v1:ConfigMap -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/core/v1, ConfigMap
- v1:Secret -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/core/v1, Secret
- v1:ServiceAccount -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/core/v1, ServiceAccount
- v1:PersistentVolumeClaim -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/core/v1, PersistentVolumeClaim
- networking.k8s.io/v1:Ingress -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/networking/v1, Ingress
- networking.k8s.io/v1:NetworkPolicy -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/networking/v1, NetworkPolicy
- rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1:Role -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/rbac/v1, Role
- rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1:ClusterRole -> cue.dev/x/k8s.io/api/rbac/v1, ClusterRole

DEFAULT METADATA:
- SchemaType: kubernetes
- ResourceType: k8s.<apiGroup>.<kind> (e.g., k8s.batch.job)
- CascadePriority: 90
- IdentityFields: [metadata.name, metadata.namespace]
- TrackedFields: Vary by resource type (e.g., status.succeeded for Jobs)

PATTERN REGISTRATION:
Create RegisterKubernetesPatterns(r *Registry) function that adds K8s pattern

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

- TypeExtractor correctly combines apiVersion:kind
- ImportMapper handles all listed resource types

title: Integrate type detection into import flow
description:
Modify import flow to use type detection when inference fails and auto-generate schemas.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- internal/importer/importer.go - Main import logic
- internal/inference/inference.go - Add hook for type detection

INTEGRATION POINT:
In Importer.assignItemSchema() (line ~821), after inference fails to find good match:

Current flow:
1. inferrer.Infer(data) tries existing schemas
2. Falls back to core.#Item if no match

New flow:
1. inferrer.Infer(data) tries existing schemas
2. If confidence < threshold (e.g., 0.5) or matched catchall:
   a. Run typepattern.Registry.Detect(data)
   b. If detected, generate schema via schemagen.GenerateFromDetectedType
   c. Save schema to repository
   d. Reload inferrer schemas
   e. Re-run inference (should now match)
3. Falls back to core.#Item only if detection also fails

NEW IMPORTER FIELD:
Add typeRegistry *typepattern.Registry to Importer struct
Initialize in NewImporter with default patterns

SCHEMA GENERATION TRIGGER:
Add method to Importer:

func (i *Importer) handleUnmatchedData(
    data interface{}, 
    inferResult *inference.InferenceResult,
) (string, float64) {
    // Only trigger if inference gave low confidence or catchall
    if inferResult.Confidence >= 0.5 && !isCatchall(inferResult.Schema) {
        return inferResult.Schema, inferResult.Confidence
    }
    
    // Try type detection
    dataMap, ok := data.(map[string]interface{})
    if !ok {
        return inferResult.Schema, inferResult.Confidence
    }
    
    detected, found := i.typeRegistry.Detect(dataMap)
    if !found {
        return inferResult.Schema, inferResult.Confidence
    }
    
    // Generate and save schema
    result, err := i.schemaGen.GenerateFromDetectedType(detected, data)
    if err != nil {
        return inferResult.Schema, inferResult.Confidence
    }
    
    if err := i.schemaGen.WriteSchema(result, result.Content, false); err != nil {
        return inferResult.Schema, inferResult.Confidence
    }
    
    // Reload schemas and re-infer
    i.inferrer.Reload()
    newResult, err := i.inferrer.Infer(data, inference.InferenceHints{})
    if err != nil {
        return inferResult.Schema, inferResult.Confidence
    }
    
    return newResult.Schema, newResult.Confidence
}

VERBOSE OUTPUT:
When auto-generating, print:
  Detected [ecosystem] resource: [typeID]
  Generated schema: [filepath]

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Type detection runs when inference gives low confidence
- Schema generated and saved automatically
- Inferrer reloads and re-runs inference
- Original data gets assigned to new schema
- Works for single items and collection items
- Verbose mode shows detection and generation
- Integration tests with K8s data

DEPENDS ON:
- pudl-49i (TypePattern struct and registry)
- Schema generation task (GenerateFromDetectedType)
- At least one pattern implementation (K8s recommended)

title: Add helpful comments to schema metadata fields in pudl schema new output
description:
When a user creates a schema with `pudl schema new`, the resulting schema file should include comments explaining the valid values and/or purpose of the following pudl metadata fields:

**Fields to document:**
- `schema_type` - valid values and when to use each
- `resource_type` - format and purpose
- `cascade_priority` - how priority values work
- `cascade_fallback` - valid formats and examples  
- `compliance_level` - valid values and their implications

**Location:** The comments should appear inline in the generated `_pudl` metadata block in the CUE schema file.

**Files involved:**
- `internal/schemagen/generator.go` - `generateCUEContent` function (lines 238-263) writes the _pudl metadata block
- `internal/review/schema.cue.tmpl` - template used by schema review workflow

**Example output (illustrative):**
```cue
_pudl: {
    schema_type: "base"  // Valid values: "base", "collection", "enriched"
    resource_type: "ec2.instance"  // Format: <package>.<definition_name>
    cascade_priority: 100  // Higher values = higher priority (0-1000)
    cascade_fallback: ["pudl.unknown.#CatchAll"]  // Schemas to try if this one doesn't match
    identity_fields: ["InstanceId"]
    tracked_fields: ["State", "Tags"]
    compliance_level: "strict"  // Valid values: "strict", "warn", "permissive"
}
```

title: Add JSON output mode for all commands
description:
Add --output/-o flag to all commands for machine-readable output, enabling scripting and automation.

Implementation:
1. Add persistent flag to cmd/root.go: --output/-o with values 'text', 'json', 'yaml'
2. Create internal/ui/output.go with OutputWriter struct

Benefits:

Confidence: 90% | Effort: Medium | Impact: High

title: Implement GitLab CI type pattern
description:
Implement GitLab CI type detection pattern in internal/typepattern/gitlab.go.

DETECTION LOGIC:
GitLab CI pipelines have characteristic structure:
- Top-level keys that are job names (objects with script field)
- Optional: stages, variables, default, workflow, include

RequiredFields strategy: Look for job-like structures
- Any top-level key that is an object containing script, stage, or extends

Alternative detection:
- Has stages array AND at least one object with script field

TYPE EXTRACTION:
GitLab CI is a single type: gitlab-ci:Pipeline

IMPORT MAPPING:
- ImportPath: cue.dev/x/gitlab/gitlabci
- Definition: Pipeline (maps to gitlabci.#Pipeline)

DEFAULT METADATA:
- SchemaType: cicd
- ResourceType: gitlab.pipeline
- CascadePriority: 85
- IdentityFields: [] (pipelines dont have obvious identity)
- TrackedFields: [stages]

PATTERN REGISTRATION:
Create RegisterGitLabPatterns(r *Registry) function

DETECTION CHALLENGES:
GitLab CI YAML is tricky because:
- Job names are arbitrary user-defined keys
- Need to detect presence of script in nested objects
- May need to iterate top-level keys to find job-like structures

IMPLEMENTATION HINT:
func detectGitLabCI(data map[string]interface{}) bool {
    // Check for stages array (common but optional)
    if _, hasStages := data[stages]; hasStages {
        // Look for job definitions
        for key, value := range data {
            if isGitLabJob(value) {
                return true
            }
        }
    }
    // Check for jobs with script directly
    jobCount := 0
    for _, value := range data {
        if isGitLabJob(value) {
            jobCount++
        }
    }
    return jobCount >= 1
}

func isGitLabJob(v interface{}) bool {
    obj, ok := v.(map[string]interface{})
    if !ok { return false }
    _, hasScript := obj[script]
    _, hasExtends := obj[extends]
    return hasScript || hasExtends
}

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Detect GitLab CI pipeline structure
- Handle pipelines with and without explicit stages
- TypeExtractor returns gitlab-ci:Pipeline
- ImportMapper returns gitlabci path and Pipeline definition
- Unit tests with sample GitLab CI YAML structures
- Pattern registered with Priority 70

DEPENDS ON:
- pudl-49i (TypePattern struct and registry)

title: [Epic] Pattern-Based Type Detection & Auto-Schema Generation
description:
Implement a generalized pattern-based type detection system that automatically identifies well-known data types (Kubernetes, AWS, GitLab CI, etc.) and generates PUDL schemas that extend canonical CUE schemas.

## Goal
When PUDL imports data that doesn't match any existing schema but matches a known type pattern (e.g., has apiVersion/kind/metadata for K8s), automatically generate a PUDL schema that extends the official canonical schema.

## Architecture (Option B - Pattern-Based)
Define patterns in a data structure, no adapter interface:

```go
var typePatterns = []TypePattern{
    {
        Name: "kubernetes",
        RequiredFields: []string{"apiVersion", "kind", "metadata"},
        TypeExtractor: func(data map[string]interface{}) string {
            return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", data["apiVersion"], data["kind"])
        },
        ImportMapper: k8sImportMapper,
    },
    // ... more patterns
}
```

## Workflow
1. Import data → Inference tries existing schemas
2. No match found → Run type detection patterns
3. Pattern matches → Generate schema extending canonical type
4. Save to ~/.pudl/schema/pudl/<ecosystem>/
5. Re-run inference (now matches)

## Sub-tasks
- Core type pattern infrastructure
- Kubernetes pattern implementation
- AWS pattern implementation  
- GitLab CI pattern implementation
- Integration with import flow
- CLI command for manual generation
- Tests and documentation

title: Implement AWS type patterns
description:
Implement AWS type detection patterns in internal/typepattern/aws.go.

AWS has multiple detection scenarios:

1. CLOUDFORMATION RESOURCES:
RequiredFields: Type, Properties
TypeExtractor: data[Type] -> AWS::EC2::Instance
ImportMapping: Currently no official CUE schemas, so generate standalone PUDL schema

2. AWS API RESPONSES (EC2):
RequiredFields: InstanceId, InstanceType, State
TypeExtractor: -> ec2:Instance
Default identity: InstanceId

3. AWS API RESPONSES (S3):
RequiredFields: Name, CreationDate (bucket) or Key, Size (object)
TypeExtractor: -> s3:Bucket or s3:Object
Default identity: Name or Key

4. AWS API RESPONSES (Lambda):
RequiredFields: FunctionName, FunctionArn, Runtime
TypeExtractor: -> lambda:Function
Default identity: FunctionArn

5. AWS API RESPONSES (IAM):
RequiredFields: RoleName, Arn, AssumeRolePolicyDocument (role)
TypeExtractor: -> iam:Role
Default identity: Arn

DEFAULT METADATA FOR AWS:
- SchemaType: aws
- ResourceType: aws.<service>.<resource> (e.g., aws.ec2.instance)
- CascadePriority: 85
- IdentityFields: Vary by resource (InstanceId, Arn, etc.)
- TrackedFields: Vary by resource

PATTERN REGISTRATION:
Create RegisterAWSPatterns(r *Registry) function

NOTE ON IMPORT MAPPING:
AWS does not have official CUE schemas in cue.dev registry. Options:
- Return ok=false from ImportMapper (generate standalone schema)
- Or future: integrate with AWS CloudFormation schema registry

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Detect EC2 instances, S3 buckets, Lambda functions, IAM roles
- CloudFormation resource detection
- TypeExtractor returns service:resource format
- MetadataDefaults appropriate per service
- Unit tests for each pattern
- Patterns registered with Priority 80

DEPENDS ON:
- pudl-49i (TypePattern struct and registry)
